speaker: ROSALIND
text: How say you now? Is it not past two o’clock?

speaker: ROSALIND
text: And here much Orlando.

speaker: CELIA
text: I warrant you, with pure love and troubled brain

speaker: CELIA
text: he hath ta’en his bow and arrows and is gone forth

speaker: CELIA
text: to sleep.

speaker: CELIA
text: Look who comes here.

speaker: SILVIUS
text: My errand is to you, fair youth.

speaker: SILVIUS
text: My gentle Phoebe did bid me give you this.

speaker: SILVIUS
text: I know not the contents, but as I guess

speaker: SILVIUS
text: By the stern brow and waspish action

speaker: SILVIUS
text: Which she did use as she was writing of it,

speaker: SILVIUS
text: It bears an angry tenor. Pardon me.

speaker: SILVIUS
text: I am but as a guiltless messenger.

speaker: ROSALIND
text: Patience herself would startle at this letter

speaker: ROSALIND
text: And play the swaggerer. Bear this, bear all.

speaker: ROSALIND
text: She says I am not fair, that I lack manners.

speaker: ROSALIND
text: She calls me proud, and that she could not love me

speaker: ROSALIND
text: Were man as rare as phoenix. ’Od’s my will,

speaker: ROSALIND
text: Her love is not the hare that I do hunt.

speaker: ROSALIND
text: Why writes she so to me? Well, shepherd, well,

speaker: ROSALIND
text: This is a letter of your own device.

speaker: SILVIUS
text: No, I protest. I know not the contents.

speaker: SILVIUS
text: Phoebe did write it.

speaker: ROSALIND
text: Come, come, you are a fool,

speaker: ROSALIND
text: And turned into the extremity of love.

speaker: ROSALIND
text: I saw her hand. She has a leathern hand,

speaker: ROSALIND
text: A freestone-colored hand. I verily did think

speaker: ROSALIND
text: That her old gloves were on, but ’twas her hands.

speaker: ROSALIND
text: She has a huswife’s hand—but that’s no matter.

speaker: ROSALIND
text: I say she never did invent this letter.

speaker: ROSALIND
text: This is a man’s invention, and his hand.

speaker: SILVIUS
text: Sure it is hers.

speaker: ROSALIND
text: Why, ’tis a boisterous and a cruel style,

speaker: ROSALIND
text: A style for challengers. Why, she defies me

speaker: ROSALIND
text: Like Turk to Christian. Women’s gentle brain

speaker: ROSALIND
text: Could not drop forth such giant-rude invention,

speaker: ROSALIND
text: Such Ethiop words, blacker in their effect

speaker: ROSALIND
text: Than in their countenance. Will you hear the letter?

speaker: SILVIUS
text: So please you, for I never heard it yet,

speaker: SILVIUS
text: Yet heard too much of Phoebe’s cruelty.

speaker: ROSALIND
text: She Phoebes me. Mark how the tyrant writes.

speaker: ROSALIND
text: Art thou god to shepherd turned,

speaker: ROSALIND
text: That a maiden’s heart hath burned?

speaker: ROSALIND
text: Can a woman rail thus?

speaker: SILVIUS
text: Call you this railing?

speaker: ROSALIND
text: Why, thy godhead laid apart,

speaker: ROSALIND
text: Warr’st thou with a woman’s heart?

speaker: ROSALIND
text: Did you ever hear such railing?

speaker: ROSALIND
text: Whiles the eye of man did woo me,

speaker: ROSALIND
text: That could do no vengeance to me.

speaker: ROSALIND
text: Meaning me a beast.

speaker: ROSALIND
text: If the scorn of your bright eyne

speaker: ROSALIND
text: Have power to raise such love in mine,

speaker: ROSALIND
text: Alack, in me what strange effect

speaker: ROSALIND
text: Would they work in mild aspect?

speaker: ROSALIND
text: Whiles you chid me, I did love.

speaker: ROSALIND
text: How then might your prayers move?

speaker: ROSALIND
text: He that brings this love to thee

speaker: ROSALIND
text: Little knows this love in me,

speaker: ROSALIND
text: And by him seal up thy mind

speaker: ROSALIND
text: Whether that thy youth and kind

speaker: ROSALIND
text: Will the faithful offer take

speaker: ROSALIND
text: Of me, and all that I can make,

speaker: ROSALIND
text: Or else by him my love deny,

speaker: ROSALIND
text: And then I’ll study how to die.

speaker: SILVIUS
text: Call you this chiding?

speaker: CELIA
text: Alas, poor shepherd.

speaker: ROSALIND
text: Do you pity him? No, he

speaker: ROSALIND
text: deserves no pity.—Wilt thou love such a woman?

speaker: ROSALIND
text: What, to make thee an instrument and play false

speaker: ROSALIND
text: strains upon thee? Not to be endured. Well, go your

speaker: ROSALIND
text: way to her, for I see love hath made thee a tame

speaker: ROSALIND
text: snake, and say this to her: that if she love me, I

speaker: ROSALIND
text: charge her to love thee; if she will not, I will never

speaker: ROSALIND
text: have her unless thou entreat for her. If you be a true

speaker: ROSALIND
text: lover, hence, and not a word, for here comes more

speaker: ROSALIND
text: company.

speaker: OLIVER
text: Good morrow, fair ones. Pray you, if you know,

speaker: OLIVER
text: Where in the purlieus of this forest stands

speaker: OLIVER
text: A sheepcote fenced about with olive trees?

speaker: CELIA
text: West of this place, down in the neighbor bottom;

speaker: CELIA
text: The rank of osiers by the murmuring stream

speaker: CELIA
text: Left on your right hand brings you to the place.

speaker: CELIA
text: But at this hour the house doth keep itself.

speaker: CELIA
text: There’s none within.

speaker: OLIVER
text: If that an eye may profit by a tongue,

speaker: OLIVER
text: Then should I know you by description—

speaker: OLIVER
text: Such garments, and such years. “The boy is fair,

speaker: OLIVER
text: Of female favor, and bestows himself

speaker: OLIVER
text: Like a ripe sister; the woman low

speaker: OLIVER
text: And browner than her brother.” Are not you

speaker: OLIVER
text: The owner of the house I did inquire for?

speaker: CELIA
text: It is no boast, being asked, to say we are.

speaker: OLIVER
text: Orlando doth commend him to you both,

speaker: OLIVER
text: And to that youth he calls his Rosalind

speaker: OLIVER
text: He sends this bloody napkin. Are you he?

speaker: ROSALIND
text: I am. What must we understand by this?

speaker: OLIVER
text: Some of my shame, if you will know of me

speaker: OLIVER
text: What man I am, and how, and why, and where

speaker: OLIVER
text: This handkercher was stained.

speaker: CELIA
text: I pray you tell it.

speaker: OLIVER
text: When last the young Orlando parted from you,

speaker: OLIVER
text: He left a promise to return again

speaker: OLIVER
text: Within an hour, and pacing through the forest,

speaker: OLIVER
text: Chewing the food of sweet and bitter fancy,

speaker: OLIVER
text: Lo, what befell. He threw his eye aside—

speaker: OLIVER
text: And mark what object did present itself:

speaker: OLIVER
text: Under an old oak, whose boughs were mossed with age

speaker: OLIVER
text: And high top bald with dry antiquity,

speaker: OLIVER
text: A wretched, ragged man, o’ergrown with hair,

speaker: OLIVER
text: Lay sleeping on his back. About his neck

speaker: OLIVER
text: A green and gilded snake had wreathed itself,

speaker: OLIVER
text: Who with her head, nimble in threats, approached

speaker: OLIVER
text: The opening of his mouth. But suddenly,

speaker: OLIVER
text: Seeing Orlando, it unlinked itself

speaker: OLIVER
text: And, with indented glides, did slip away

speaker: OLIVER
text: Into a bush, under which bush’s shade

speaker: OLIVER
text: A lioness, with udders all drawn dry,

speaker: OLIVER
text: Lay couching, head on ground, with catlike watch

speaker: OLIVER
text: When that the sleeping man should stir—for ’tis

speaker: OLIVER
text: The royal disposition of that beast

speaker: OLIVER
text: To prey on nothing that doth seem as dead.

speaker: OLIVER
text: This seen, Orlando did approach the man

speaker: OLIVER
text: And found it was his brother, his elder brother.

speaker: CELIA
text: O, I have heard him speak of that same brother,

speaker: CELIA
text: And he did render him the most unnatural

speaker: CELIA
text: That lived amongst men.

speaker: OLIVER
text: And well he might so do,

speaker: OLIVER
text: For well I know he was unnatural.

speaker: ROSALIND
text: But to Orlando: did he leave him there,

speaker: ROSALIND
text: Food to the sucked and hungry lioness?

speaker: OLIVER
text: Twice did he turn his back and purposed so,

speaker: OLIVER
text: But kindness, nobler ever than revenge,

speaker: OLIVER
text: And nature, stronger than his just occasion,

speaker: OLIVER
text: Made him give battle to the lioness,

speaker: OLIVER
text: Who quickly fell before him; in which hurtling,

speaker: OLIVER
text: From miserable slumber I awaked.

speaker: CELIA
text: Are you his brother?

speaker: ROSALIND
text: Was ’t you he rescued?

speaker: CELIA
text: Was ’t you that did so oft contrive to kill him?

speaker: OLIVER
text: ’Twas I, but ’tis not I. I do not shame

speaker: OLIVER
text: To tell you what I was, since my conversion

speaker: OLIVER
text: So sweetly tastes, being the thing I am.

speaker: ROSALIND
text: But for the bloody napkin?

speaker: OLIVER
text: By and by.

speaker: OLIVER
text: When from the first to last betwixt us two

speaker: OLIVER
text: Tears our recountments had most kindly bathed—

speaker: OLIVER
text: As how I came into that desert place—

speaker: OLIVER
text: In brief, he led me to the gentle duke,

speaker: OLIVER
text: Who gave me fresh array and entertainment,

speaker: OLIVER
text: Committing me unto my brother’s love;

speaker: OLIVER
text: Who led me instantly unto his cave,

speaker: OLIVER
text: There stripped himself, and here upon his arm

speaker: OLIVER
text: The lioness had torn some flesh away,

speaker: OLIVER
text: Which all this while had bled; and now he fainted,

speaker: OLIVER
text: And cried in fainting upon Rosalind.

speaker: OLIVER
text: Brief, I recovered him, bound up his wound,

speaker: OLIVER
text: And after some small space, being strong at heart,

speaker: OLIVER
text: He sent me hither, stranger as I am,

speaker: OLIVER
text: To tell this story, that you might excuse

speaker: OLIVER
text: His broken promise, and to give this napkin

speaker: OLIVER
text: Dyed in his blood unto the shepherd youth

speaker: OLIVER
text: That he in sport doth call his Rosalind.

speaker: CELIA
text: Why, how now, Ganymede, sweet Ganymede?

speaker: OLIVER
text: Many will swoon when they do look on blood.

speaker: CELIA
text: There is more in it.—Cousin Ganymede.

speaker: OLIVER
text: Look, he recovers.

speaker: ROSALIND
text: I would I were at home.

speaker: CELIA
text: We’ll lead you thither.—I pray you,

speaker: CELIA
text: will you take him by the arm?

speaker: OLIVER
text: Be of good cheer,

speaker: OLIVER
text: youth. You a man? You lack a man’s heart.

speaker: ROSALIND
text: I do so, I confess it. Ah,

speaker: ROSALIND
text: sirrah, a body would think this was well-counterfeited.

speaker: ROSALIND
text: I pray you tell your brother how well I

speaker: ROSALIND
text: counterfeited. Heigh-ho.

speaker: OLIVER
text: This was not counterfeit. There is too great

speaker: OLIVER
text: testimony in your complexion that it was a passion

speaker: OLIVER
text: of earnest.

speaker: ROSALIND
text: Counterfeit, I assure you.

speaker: OLIVER
text: Well then, take a good heart, and counterfeit to

speaker: OLIVER
text: be a man.

speaker: ROSALIND
text: So I do; but, i’ faith, I should

speaker: ROSALIND
text: have been a woman by right.

speaker: CELIA
text: Come, you look paler and paler. Pray

speaker: CELIA
text: you draw homewards.—Good sir, go with us.

speaker: OLIVER
text: That will I, for I must bear answer back

speaker: OLIVER
text: How you excuse my brother, Rosalind.

speaker: ROSALIND
text: I shall devise something.

speaker: ROSALIND
text: But I pray you commend my counterfeiting to him.

speaker: ROSALIND
text: Will you go?
